trade.summary: Germany is the UK's largest EU trading partner. Trade volumes declined post-Brexit due to customs friction and regulatory divergence. Germany exports vehicles, machinery, and chemicals; the UK exports financial services, pharmaceuticals, and whisky.

diplomatic.summary: Relations were strained by Brexit negotiations but have gradually improved. The Windsor Framework stabilized the Northern Ireland issue. Germany has been more pragmatic than some EU members in pursuing UK reset. Both cooperate closely in NATO and on Ukraine support.

military.summary: Both NATO members with deep defense cooperation. The UK and Germany coordinate closely on Ukraine military assistance. Joint NATO exercises are frequent. Germany participates in UK-led NATO battlegroup rotations in Estonia and other Eastern flank operations.

financial.summary: Post-Brexit financial linkages reduced. London remains Europe's dominant financial hub despite Brexit. German firms maintain significant UK investment; UK firms have partially relocated European operations to Germany (Frankfurt).

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. North Sea gas interconnectors provide some linkage. Both are transitioning aggressively to renewables. Offshore wind cooperation through North Sea agreements.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific ties, though EU funding mechanisms for the UK were disrupted post-Brexit. The UK rejoined Horizon Europe in 2024, restoring collaborative grant access. Academic exchanges remain robust.
